theme: Claim Hygiene, Worktree Safety & Loop Planner Context

shots.title: Fix stale claims: session cleanup parity + expiry filtering
shots.notes: SQLite removeSession/cleanStaleSessions now clean claims (parity with PG). getActiveClaims filters expired claims in both stores. Next-action guard scopes claims to current session.

shots.title: worktree-self-remove guard: block git worktree remove on own cwd
shots.notes: New guard following worktree-merge pattern. Resolves target path, compares to cwd, blocks with ExitWorktree guidance.

shots.title: slope worktree cleanup CLI command

hazards.description: Unused function getMainWorktreePath left in initial implementation

shots.notes: New command with --path, --all, --dry-run. Parses git worktree list --porcelain, checks PR merge status via gh, graceful fallback if gh unavailable.

shots.title: Inject hazards + similar-ticket context into loop prompt (--lite fallback)
shots.notes: Added --lite flag to slope prep. Outputs hazards + similar tickets without embedding index. Injected as fallback in run.sh.

shots.title: Pass prep-plan-discovered files as Aider --file flags
shots.notes: run.sh now calls slope prep --json, extracts files via jq, adds as --file flags. Deduplicates against TICKET_PRIMARY_FILES.

conditions: Worked in worktree isolation while another session was active on main
Deep review (3 rounds) completed before implementation — 12 findings total
All 5 tickets implemented with test coverage, build + typecheck + tests passing
special_plays: Used newly-created slope worktree cleanup command to clean stale worktrees immediately after implementation
3-round deep review caught PG/SQLite parity gap, cleanStaleSessions claim leak, and fragile markdown parsing — all fixed in plan before coding
yardage_book_updates: SQLite store now has full claim lifecycle parity with PG store
worktree-self-remove guard protects against shell-breaking worktree removal
slope worktree cleanup available for branch/worktree housekeeping
slope prep --lite provides embedding-free hazard context fallback
course_management_notes: Review found 1 minor code quality issue (unused function) — removed before merge
Architect review confirmed plan-to-implementation fidelity was high
slope_factors: Deep plan review (3 rounds, 12 findings) de-risked implementation significantly
Existing guard pattern (worktree-merge) made S64-2 straightforward
PG store already had correct claim cleanup — SQLite just needed parity
